title: AI/Quantum Investment Ledger — government commitment record
description: One row per announced government (or government-adjacent) AI/quantum investment commitment. The ledger is the product; the index is an optional layer above it. Every figure is tagged on the comparability axes BEFORE any normalization or aggregation. Public and private/mobilized capital are stored as SEPARATE fields and are NEVER summed into a single 'government investment' number.

iso3.description: ISO-3166 alpha-3, or 'EU' for the bloc. Used to join normalization denominators (GDP, population, GBARD).

domain.description: Investment domain. 'semiconductor' is AI-adjacent and flagged so it can be filtered in/out.

headline_amount.description: Headline figure in ORIGINAL currency units (full units, not millions/billions). Null when only a range is given.

amount_low.description: Low end for vague figures ('up to', 'as much as'), original currency. Null if a point figure.

amount_high.description: High end for vague figures, original currency. Null if a point figure.

currency.description: ISO 4217 of the headline figure (USD, EUR, CNY, CAD, GBP, ...).

usd_approx.description: Headline converted to USD at (ideally) announcement-date FX. The comparable headline-stock figure.

fx_rate_to_usd.description: FX rate (1 unit of currency -> USD) used for usd_approx. Null = pending (Stage 2 pins per-record announcement-date rates).

horizon_end_year.description: Null = open-ended. With start_year, lets the generator annualize (usd_approx / years) for flow comparisons while preserving the headline stock.

announced.description: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM of the announcement.

actor_type.description: Nature of the money. 'government_appropriated' = passed a budget; 'mobilization_target' / 'private' = headline is mostly NOT a public outlay.

public_outlay_usd.description: Genuinely-public component of the headline, USD. Null = unknown/unspecified.

private_mobilized_usd.description: Private / 'mobilized' component, USD. Stored separately; NEVER added to public_outlay_usd to form a government figure.

verification_status.description: verified = traced to a primary budget/official doc; reported = credible press, URL may be pending; estimate = third-party modelled; unconfirmed = no reliable primary source.

source_url.description: Primary-source URL. Empty/null = pending verification (verification_status should then be 'reported' at best).

source_tier.description: Fidelity of the CITED source, independent of verification_status. 1 = primary/official (gov budget docs, ministry/.gov/EC press releases, legislative appropriations, official program pages, primary corporate filings for private commitments); 2 = major established secondary (wire services + papers of record — Reuters/AP/Bloomberg/FT/WSJ/NYT/Nikkei/Economist — and established research trackers: OECD.AI, Stanford HAI, McKinsey, WIPO, Epoch AI, CSIS); 3 = trade press / regional outlet / news aggregator (discovery-grade, not citable on its own); 4 = low fidelity (PR wires, social posts, anonymous blogs, content farms). THE COLLECTION GATE keeps only tier <= 2 — see _collection_scope. Null = unassigned (legacy rows; scanned rows must carry a tier).

event_key.description: Stable key grouping re-announcements of the SAME money across summits, for dedup. Sum at most one record per event_key.

tradable_share.description: Fraction (0-1) of the commitment that is globally-priced tradable goods (compute/GPUs/hardware), converted at MARKET FX; the remainder (talent/operations/local construction) is non-tradable and converted at PPP in the blended view. Null = use the generator's domain-default map (compute 0.90, semiconductor 0.65, ai 0.55, ai+quantum 0.50, quantum 0.45). Per-record override when line-item detail is known.

notes.description: Caveats: bundling, overlap with other records, opacity, etc.

views.absolute_usd: usd_approx (headline stock) and annualized_usd (stock / horizon years)
views.per_gbard_x: annualized_usd / gbard_usd (multiple of annual government R&D budget; null where gbard unknown)

currency_basis.fx: usd_approx as stored (market FX) — appropriate for tradable compute/hardware
currency_basis.ppp_blended: tradable_share at FX + (1 - tradable_share) at PPP, where USD_ppp = usd_approx / price_level_index. PPP is a SENSITIVITY SCENARIO, not ground truth.

_realization.description: Stage 3 — commitment -> outlay reconciliation. A separate append-only file (realizations.jsonl) holds dated observations of how much of each pledge has actually been realized, keyed by event_key. The generator joins them to the ledger and computes per-record realization_rate, expected_rate, and pace_status. Realization is an EVENT-level fact (records sharing an event_key share the computed values) and nothing is summed across events.

event_key.description: Must match an event_key in government-commitments.jsonl. Orphan keys are flagged by the generator.

as_of.description: YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD of the observation. Observations are sorted by as_of; the latest drives the displayed figure.

realized_usd.description: Cumulative realized amount in USD as of this date. Null when no credible dollar figure exists yet — then pace_flag must be set.

realized_basis.description: What 'realized' means here. obligated = awarded/announced but not paid; disbursed = public cash out the door; deployed = private/SWF capital actually spent; reported = press characterization only. obligated is NOT disbursed — always surfaced in the viewer.

pace_flag.description: Manual pace override, used when realization can't be expressed as a dollar figure (e.g. a credibly-reported slow start). When set it overrides the computed pace; when null the generator derives pace from realized-vs-expected.

computed_fields.realization_rate: latest realized_usd / committed (the event's max usd_approx across its ledger records). Null if no realized_usd.
computed_fields.expected_rate: min(1, max(0, (as_of_year - horizon_start_year) / (horizon_end_year - horizon_start_year))) — fraction due by the observation date on a linear schedule. Null if the horizon is open-ended.
computed_fields.pace_status: pace_flag if set; else from realization_rate / expected_rate: >=1.1 ahead, >=0.9 on_track, >=0.5 behind, else stalled.

_source_tiers.description: Source-fidelity taxonomy for the source_tier field. Fidelity (how authoritative the OUTLET is) is a separate axis from verification_status (whether the CLAIM has been traced to a primary doc). A tier-1 official release can still be verification_status 'reported' until a human pins it; a tier-2 wire report is credible but secondary.

tiers.1: Primary / official — government budget documents, legislative appropriations, ministry/.gov/European-Commission press releases, official program/initiative pages, central-bank or treasury releases; for private commitments, the primary corporate filing or official newsroom (e.g. an SEC filing, an official company announcement).
tiers.2: Major established secondary — international wire services and papers of record (Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, Financial Times, Wall Street Journal, New York Times, Nikkei, The Economist) and established research institutions / trackers (OECD.AI, Stanford HAI AI Index, McKinsey, WIPO, Epoch AI, CSIS, major national academies).
tiers.3: Trade press / regional / aggregator — industry trade outlets, smaller regional press, editorially-staffed tech blogs (TechCrunch, The Register), and news aggregators (Google News surfacing). Useful for DISCOVERY but not citable on its own.
tiers.4: Low fidelity — unvetted PR-wire distribution, social-media posts, anonymous blogs, SEO/content-farm sites, rumor.

_source_tiers.gate: The daily collection routine keeps only source_tier <= 2 (the tier-2 fidelity gate). Tier-3 sources may surface that an announcement EXISTS, but the candidate's cited source must be tier <= 2 before it enters the review queue; tier-3/4-only items are dropped by the sweep and rejected by ingest.py (override: --allow-low-tier, for hand-curated rows).

_collection_scope.description: What the announcement collection aims to be COMPLETE over.
_collection_scope.in_scope: Every announcement that (a) concerns AI, quantum, ai+quantum, AI-enabling compute, or AI-relevant semiconductor capacity, AND (b) involves a country or bloc — i.e. is a government/government-adjacent commitment, OR a private/mobilization commitment whose locus is a national jurisdiction (national AI champions, sovereign-AI data centers, summit pledges). Public and private money are BOTH collected, kept in separate fields, and NEVER summed (the cardinal rule).
_collection_scope.out_of_scope: Pure private company funding rounds with no national/sovereign framing; product launches; opinion/market-size pieces; academic results without a funding commitment.
_collection_scope.delineation: Each record carries actor_type + the public_outlay_usd / private_mobilized_usd split so the public-vs-private boundary is explicit on every row, exactly as in the rest of the ledger.
_collection_scope.target: All-time, append-only; Stage-1 advance benchmark >= 40 jurisdictions with >= 3 tier<=2 sourced records each.
